title: AvidXchange Announces Second-Quarter 2025 Financial Results
author: Avidxchange Holdings, Inc.
description: AvidXchange reported Q2 2025 financial results with total revenue of $110.6 million, a 5.2% year-over-year increase. The company experienced a net loss of $9.5 million and is pending acquisition by TPG in partnership with Corpay.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Mixed financial performance with revenue growth but net loss, and pending acquisition indicates transitional period
Actively pursuing acquisition of AvidXchange, indicating strategic interest in the company
Partnering with TPG to acquire AvidXchange, suggesting strategic growth opportunity

title: AppLovin, First Solar And Li Auto Are Among Top 10 Large Cap Losers Last Week (June 16-20): Are The Others In Your Portfolio?
author: Mohit Manghnani
description: Several large-cap stocks, including AppLovin, First Solar, and Li Auto, experienced significant losses last week, with AppLovin and First Solar declining over 13% due to a short-seller report and reduced renewable energy incentives, respectively.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: AppLovin's stock fell 14.7% last week, extending losses after a short-seller report by Culper Research.
First Solar's stock dropped 13.6% as the U.S. Senate Finance Committee's proposed tax bill slashed incentives for renewable energy, hitting solar stocks broadly.
Arista Networks' stock declined 9.94% amid a tech sector pullback driven by rising Middle East tensions between Israel and Iran.
Corpay's stock fell 9.64% last week amid pressure from Coinbase's announcement of its new stablecoin payments stack, which may impact Corpay's business outlook.

title: Earnings Just Put Dan Loeb's Bets To The Test — Here's What Held Up
author: Lekha Gupta
description: The article discusses the earnings results of several companies in Dan Loeb's Third Point portfolio, including Cinemark Holdings, Corpay, AvidXchange Holdings, Jacobs Solutions, and Primo Brands. The article provides insights into the performance of these companies and the impact on Loeb's investments.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The company's Q1 EPS loss was worse than expected, but revenue slightly beat expectations. The outperformance was driven across key metrics, including higher attendance, improved per-patron spending, and additional revenue streams.
The company's Q1 adjusted EPS met consensus, but revenue missed. The company slightly narrowed its 2025 adjusted EPS guidance and increased its 2025 sales guidance.
The company inked a deal to be acquired by Corpay and TPG Inc. for $10 per share in cash, valuing the company at $2.2 billion.

The company reported a second-quarter 2025 revenue increase of 2.2% year-over-year, topping the consensus, and adjusted EPS that beat the consensus. The company also sees adjusted net revenue growing mid-to-high single digits, with an EBITDA margin of 13.8-14.0% and FCF conversion exceeding 100% of net income.

The company reported first-quarter revenue growth of 42.1% year-on-year, missing the analyst consensus estimate, while adjusted EPS beat the consensus estimate. For 2025, the company sees net sales growth of 3%-5% and expects adjusted EBITDA of $1.6 billion-$1.628 billion.

title: AvidXchange To Go Private For 22% Premium In M&A Deal
author: Lekha Gupta
description: AvidXchange Holdings Inc. (AVDX) has agreed to be acquired by Corpay Inc. (CPAY) and TPG Inc. (TPG) for $10 per share in cash, valuing the company at $2.2 billion. The deal represents a 22% premium over AvidXchange's previous closing price.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The company is being acquired at a 22% premium, indicating the acquirers see value in the business.
Corpay is acquiring a 33% stake in AvidXchange, indicating they see growth potential in the business.
TPG is partnering with Corpay to acquire AvidXchange, suggesting they also see value in the company.
The ETF is mentioned as a way for investors to gain exposure to AvidXchange, but no specific commentary is provided on the ETF itself.
The ETF is mentioned as a way for investors to gain exposure to AvidXchange, but no specific commentary is provided on the ETF itself.

title: US Stocks Likely To Open Higher Ahead Of Fed's Verdict On Interest Rates: 'A Short Bout Of Stagflation Is Likely,' Says Expert
author: Rishabh Mishra
description: U.S. stock futures rose ahead of the Federal Reserve's interest rate decision. Analysts expect a short bout of stagflation due to the impact of tariffs on the economy.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Arista Networks reported mixed first-quarter results, with revenue missing the consensus estimate.
Advanced Micro Devices reported stronger-than-expected first-quarter financial results and provided a positive outlook for the second quarter.
Corpay rose after AvidXchange agreed to be acquired by TPG in partnership with Corpay for $2.2 billion.
Walt Disney Co. is expected to report earnings before the opening bell, with analysts expecting a quarterly earnings of $1.20 per share on revenue of $23.14 billion.
Johnson Controls International is expected to report earnings of 80 cents per share on revenue of $5.66 billion before the opening bell.
Marvell Technology dropped after it postponed its investor day, citing the macroeconomic environment.
Owens Corning is expected to report a quarterly earnings of $2.87 per share on revenue of $2.51 billion before the opening bell.
The Invesco QQQ Trust ETF, which tracks the Nasdaq 100 index, declined in premarket trading.
The SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust, which tracks the S&P 500 index, fell in premarket trading.
Uber Technologies is expected to report earnings of 50 cents per share on revenue of $11.62 billion before the opening bell.
